name: Architext 1.6.0 Routing Overhaul
summary: Architext 1.6.0 rebuilds orthogonal edge routing around a single weighted-sum mount-cost objective, reduces and hops diagram crossings, coordinates reciprocal pairs, and derives mount spacing from the rendered arrowhead instead of magic numbers. A sanitized high-complexity corpus and a fitness ratchet lock the gains against regression.

required.title: Single weighted-sum mount-cost objective
required.summary: Replace the tiered mount cost with one weighted-sum objective ordered doglegs > crossings > crowding, plus relief passes for crowded surfaces, soft over-capacity tolerance, and optimizer move scoring by strict crossings.

required.title: Crossing reduction and hop rendering
required.summary: Vertex-hop collinear merge, width-aware spread with crossing-minimizing unit order, gutter-lane ordering (farthest target takes the outermost lane), and a combinatorial swap-search; adaptive hop radius so every rendered crossing carries a hop.

required.title: Reciprocal-pair coordination
required.summary: Pair request and return edges as parallel bundles, straighten self-crossing pairs, build nested gutter bridges, and distribute pair mounts atomically and evenly across shared faces.

required.title: Arrowhead-derived mount spacing
required.summary: Derive the minimum legible gap and surface capacity from the rendered arrowhead (half an arrowhead, 4px) instead of two disagreeing magic numbers, so reciprocal pairs stop splitting onto remote faces when they would have fit.

required.title: High-complexity corpus and fitness ratchet
required.summary: A sanitized in-repo routing corpus (66 nodes, 30 views, 15 flows) plus a frozen-baseline fitness ratchet and pair-internal and lane-order crossing detectors, wired into the benchmark gate so no change can silently make a complex diagram worse.

required.title: Planning overlay narration and viewer fixes
required.summary: Narrate routing passes in the planning overlay, persist the steps-panel collapse state, keep overlays at the canvas z-index and fit them to content bounds, and restore the TypeScript build after the overlay change.

deferred.title: Repository tree view
deferred.summary: Top-level Repo Tree tab for source layout understanding. Reassigned to 1.6.1 Repository Intelligence and Diagram Config; not part of the routing overhaul shipped in 1.6.0.

deferred.title: Repository blast-radius search
deferred.summary: Search for filenames, components, or code concepts to see where a concern reaches. Reassigned to 1.6.1 Repository Intelligence and Diagram Config.

deferred.title: Diagram spacing controls
deferred.summary: Explicit controls for diagram column width and sequence-diagram row spacing. Reassigned to 1.6.1.

deferred.title: Persistent architecture element notes
deferred.summary: Edit or annotate architecture elements and persist the notes in Architext JSON. Reassigned to 1.6.1.

workstreams.name: Orthogonal Routing Engine
workstreams.summary: Rebuild orthogonal edge routing on a single weighted-sum mount-cost objective with crossing reduction, reciprocal-pair coordination, and arrowhead-derived spacing.

workstreams.name: Routing Validation Harness
workstreams.summary: Lock routing quality with an in-repo high-complexity corpus and a frozen-baseline fitness ratchet in the benchmark gate.

workstreams.name: Viewer Routing Ergonomics
workstreams.summary: Surface routing passes in the planning overlay and tidy viewer overlay behavior without changing semantic data.

evidence.label: release:check green (330/330 tests, benchmarks, corpus ratchet, build, UAT, pack smoke)
